citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Cache-hit will be observable on the next CI run over an unchanged pyproject.toml (operator-attested AC).' — future-tense, no specific run cited.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] A repeat CI run on an unchanged `pyproject.toml` logs a pip cache hit (the `setup-python` "restore cache" step reports a cache restore), recorded as a comment citing the specific run.
findings.detail: The ticket comment explicitly states the cache hit 'will be observable on the next CI run over an unchanged pyproject.toml' — this is a forward-looking statement, not an attestation of an observed outcome. No specific CI run ID, run URL, or log excerpt showing a cache restore is cited. The concrete attestation required (a reference to a specific run where the cache hit was observed) is absent.
findings.remediation: After the next CI run on an unchanged pyproject.toml completes, record a ticket comment that names the specific run URL or run ID, identifies the job/step where the 'setup-python' restore-cache step reported a cache hit (with the log line or screenshot), and the timestamp of observation.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
